title: Ambient light probe
abstract: The examples relate to a method and apparatus to measuring ambient light information that is used in the manipulation or augmentation of an image presented on a display. The apparatus measures ambient light characteristic information from at least one direction in an environment in which the apparatus is located. The measured ambient light characteristic enable substantially real time generation and application of an image effect to an image presented on a display device.
claims:
Claims What is Claimed Is: 1. An light probe, comprising: a housing having a surface with a perimeter; a first sensor group comprising a plurality of light sensors, wherein: individual light sensors of the first sensor group are positioned in predetermined locations about the perimeter of the housing surface and oriented to receive ambient light from different respective directions; each of the light sensors of the first sensor group is configured to output a light intensity value in response to ambient light received at the respective predetermined location; a second sensor group comprising one or more light sensors positioned at a central position of the housing, wherein the second sensor group is configured to output color component values in response to ambient light received by the one or more light sensors at the central position of the housing; and a communication interface coupled to the first sensor group and the second sensor group, wherein the communication interface is configured to output light information representing the light intensity values and the color component values. 2. The light probe of claim 1 , further comprising: a processor, coupled to the sensor groups and the communication interface, configured to: receive the light intensity values from the first sensor group; attach a direction value to each of the received light intensity values based on the respective individual lights sensor of the first sensor group that provided the received light intensity values; receive the color component values from the second sensor group; and format the received light intensity values with attached direction values and the received color component values for output as a portion of the light information via the communication interface. 3. The light probe of claim 1 , wherein the perimeter of the housing comprises: three sides, each of the three sides being slanted at a predetermined angle relative to a reference surface; and 

 each of the three sides includes the predetermined location at which a respective light sensor of the first group is located. 4. The light probe of claim 1 , the first sensor group further comprising: a first light sensor of the plurality of individual light sensors of the first sensor group having a sensor field of view axis that is perpendicular to a surface of the housing at the predetermined location at which the first light sensor is positioned; a second light sensor of the plurality of individual light sensors of the first sensor group having a sensor field of view axis that is perpendicular to a surface of the housing at the predetermined location at which the second light sensor of the first sensor group is positioned; and a third of the plurality of individual light sensors of the first sensor group having a sensor field of view axis that is perpendicular to a surface of the housing at the predetermined location at which the third light sensor of the first sensor group is positioned. 5. The light probe of claim 1, the second sensor group having a sensor field of view axis perpendicular to a surface of the housing at the predetermined location at which at least one sensor of the second sensor group is positioned. 6. The light probe of claim 1 , wherein the color component values output by the one or more light sensors of the second sensor group are individual intensity values of different light colors measured in response to the ambient light received by the one or more light sensors at the central position of the housing. 7. The light probe of claim 1, wherein the one or more light sensors of the second group positioned at the central position of the housing are configured about or at least substantially parallel to a central axis of the housing perpendicular to a reference surface. 8. A light probe, comprising: ambient light sensors; a housing to which the ambient light sensors are attached at predetermined locations about a perimeter of the housing relative to an axis perpendicular to a reference surface, wherein: the ambient light sensors are attached to the housing at the predetermined locations about the axis and oriented to receive and measure ambient light from different directions around the axis; and 

 circuitry, coupled to the ambient light sensors, to produce directional light data based on the measurements by the ambient light sensors, wherein the produced directional light data is at least one of ambient light intensity data or ambient light color characteristic data. 9. The light probe of claim 8, wherein the circuitry is an analog-to-digital converter and a processor, wherein when the produced directional light data is ambient light intensity data includes an indication of a direction associated with the at least one ambient light intensity sensor. 10. The light probe of claim 9, wherein the produced ambient light color characteristic data includes an indication of a directions associated with the ambient light color characteristic sensor. 11. The light probe of claim 8, wherein the at least one ambient light sensor comprises: a first, a second and a third ambient light intensity sensor, wherein: each of the first, second and third ambient light intensity sensors is attached at a respective predetermined location about the axis; each of the first, second and third ambient light intensity sensors are oriented to receive and measure ambient light intensity from three respective different directions; and each of the first, second and third ambient light intensity sensor produces directional light intensity data based on the light intensity measured at the respective predetermined locations about the axis. 12. A light sensing system, comprising: a light probe comprising: directional light intensity sensors configured to measure light intensity of ambient light received from more than one direction with respect to a center point of the light probe; and a color component sensor configured to measure color components of the ambient light; an image display device that presents an image; a processing system, in or in communication with the image display device, comprising: a memory; 

 a processor coupled to the memory and the light probe and coupled to control operation of the image display device; and prograinrning in the memory, wherein execution of the programming by the processor configures the system to perform functions including functions to: obtain a selected image for output via the display device; obtain ambient light information measured by the light probe; modify the selected image by applying an image effect that is generated at least in response to the ambient light information; and control operation of the image display device to present the modified image. 13. The lighting device of claim 12, wherein the image display device is a display device selected from a group consisting of: an organic light emitting diode display device, a non-organic light emitting diode display device, a plasma display device, and a liquid crystal display device. 14. The lighting device of claim 12, wherein the selected image selection is an image of a luminaire, a landscape, a sky scene, other real world scene, a graphic object, a real object, a video, a video segment and an art installation. 15. The lighting device of claim 12, further comprising a communication interface coupled to an external source and to the processor system; and, wherein when the selected image is an image of a luminaire, the processor is further configured to: receive configuration data from a source external, wherein the configuration data includes the selected image of the luminaire and a general lighting distribution selection; store the configuration data in the memory; control the image display device to: present the image of the luminaire, and generate a general lighting distribution based on the general lighting distribution selection. 16. A method, comprising: presenting on a display device located in a premises an image of an object; 

 measuring an intensity of the ambient light in the premises by a first sensor of a probe, the first sensor being positioned and oriented in a first direction with reference to a centra] axis of the probe; measuring an intensity of the ambient light in the premises by a second sensor of the probe positioned and oriented in a second direction with reference to the central axis of the probe; measuring an intensity of the ambient light in the premises by a third sensor of the probe positioned and oriented in a third direction with reference to the central axis of the probe, wherein the first, second and third directions are different from one another; measuring color components of the ambient light by a color component sensor of the probe; providing, in real time, ambient light intensity values based on the measurements by the first, second and third sensors and direction values indicating the direction from which the ambient light intensity value was measured; and providing, in real time, color component values of the ambient color components based on the measurements by the color component sensor; generating, by a processor, an image effect utilizing the provided ambient light intensity values, direction values and color component values that accounts for the changes of ambient light in the premises; and applying the generated image effect to the image of the object presented on the display device to provide a modified image, wherein the applied generated image effect alters an appearance of the image on the display device based on changes to ambient light in the premises. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the color component sensor is oriented in a fourth direction different from the first, second and third directions. 18. The method of claim 16, further comprising: prior to measuring an intensity of the ambient light in a premises, identifying an orientation of the first direction of the first sensor, the second direction of the second sensor and the third direction of the third sensor of the probe by referencing an orientation of the image presented on the display device. 19. The method of claim 16, further comprising: 

 prior to measuring an intensity of the ambient light in a premises, establishing a connection via a network between the display device and the probe, wherein the probe further comprises the processor. 20. The method of claim 16, further comprising: prior to measuring an intensity of the ambient light in a premises, establishing a connection via a network between the processor, the display device and the probe, wherein the processor is located remote from the probe and the display device. 21. The method of claim 16, further comprising: after passage of a predetermined time, repeating each of the measuring, providing, generating and applying steps. 22. A method, comprising: measuring, by a light probe, ambient light in a lighting environment rom four different directions; outputting to a processor the measured ambient light as light information, wherein the light information comprises light intensity values of the measured ambient light from at least three of the four directions and color component values of the measured ambient light from a fourth of the four directions; generating, by the processor, an image effect using the received light information; and applying the image effect to an image of an object presented on a display device. 23. A light probe, comprising: a plurality of ambient light sensors, wherein each of the plurality of ambient light sensors measures a characteristic of ambient light; a housing having a number of predetermined locations at which respective individual light sensors of the plurality of light sensors are positioned; and circuitry, coupled to the ambient light sensors, to produce light characteristic information based on the measurements by the light sensors and an indication of a direction from which the ambient light characteristic is measured. 24. The light probe of claim 23, wherein: a first group of the plurality of ambient light sensors measures light characteristics in a first radial direction relative to a central vertical axis of the housing, and 

 a second group of the plurality of ambient light sensors measures light characteristics in a second radial direction relative to the central vertical axis of the housing, the first radial direction is different from the second radial direction. 25. The light probe of claim 23, wherein: a first group of the plurality of ambient light sensors measures light characteristics in one or more radial directions relative to a central vertical axis of the housing, and a second group of the plurality of ambient light sensors measures light characteristics in a direction of the central vertical axis of the housing. 26. The light probe of claim 23 , wherein: the plurality of ambient light sensors include at least one ambient light intensity sensor and at least one ambient light color characteristic sensor, and the light characteristic information includes directional light intensity data based on light intensity measured by the at least one ambient light intensity sensor and ambient light color characteristic data based on light color characteristics measured by the ambient light color characteristic sensor. 27. The light probe of claim 23, wherein: the plurality of ambient light sensors include at least one ambient light color characteristic sensor; and the light characteristic information includes directional light intensity data of a color of light based on light color characteristics measured by the ambient light color characteristic sensor. 28. The light probe of claim 23, wherein: the housing is any N-sided polygon; and the ambient light sensors are attached to the housing at predetermined sides about the vertical axis and oriented to receive and measure ambient light from different directions around the axis of the housing. 29. The light probe of claim 23, wherein the circuitry is configured to: output the light characteristic information from each of the plurality of ambient light sensors, wherein the light information comprises light intensity values of the measured ambient light and the indication of the direction from which the ambient light was measured. 30. The light probe of claim 29, wherein when the plurality of ambient light sensors includes an ambient light color characteristic light sensor, the light information from the ambient light color characteristic light sensor further comprises color component values of the measured ambient light. 31. The light probe of claim 29, further comprising: a memory; programming code stored in the memory; a processor, when executing the program code stored in the memory, is configured to perform functions, including functions to: in response to receiving the outputted light characteristic information, generate an image effect using the received light characteristic information; and applying the image effect to an image of an object presented on a display device.
